sites.name: STL Dispatcher
sites.description: Consolidated periodic-task Worker — merges supabase-keepalive and stl-status-sync under one cron trigger to stay within Cloudflare's 5-slot free-tier cap. Keepalive is heartbeat-gated (fires effectively every 5 days despite a daily cron). Full pipeline verified live Jul 6: keepalive.ok:true, statusSync.ok:true (GitHub reconciliation committing real diffs), both tasks genuinely healthy, not just returning 200.
features: Supabase keepalive ping — real table query (stl_issues), gated by STATUS_KV heartbeat, real cadence ~5 days

runStatusSync() reconciles data.json against live CF state (cron drift, phantom Workers, heartbeat staleness) and commits changes to pdluker/stl-status via GitHub Contents API, with a SHA concurrency guard
GET /health — unauthenticated, reports last-success + staleness for both tasks
POST /trigger — Authorization: Bearer gated, manual immediate run for testing
Frees 1 CF cron slot vs. the two Workers it replaces
sites.automation: Fully automated — single daily cron drives both tasks, both verified genuinely healthy (not false-positive)

openTasks.title: fire-api keepalive ping never activated in stl-dispatcher
openTasks.detail: Original supabase-keepalive Worker was verified (Jun 30) to also touch fire-api / crash_readings. That ping line exists in dispatcher.js runKeepalive() but is COMMENTED OUT — only the Supabase stl_issues query is active/verified as of Jul 6. If fire-api liveness matters, uncomment and verify it separately; don't assume it's covered just because keepalive.ok:true.

recentActivity.event: Security Insights review completed (30 findings, down from 33 Jul 12) — confirmed barrettspto/PTO tracker fully decommissioned (Worker + DNS gone), confirmed familyplate.pages.dev fully decommissioned, confirmed tripcompanion.pages.dev fully decommissioned. All three removed from this dashboard's site list.

HSTS fixed on fire-intel + memory-lattice Pages projects via public/_headers file (zone-wide HSTS toggle doesn't cover *.pages.dev hostnames). memory-lattice required a Vite dependency rollback first — npm audit fix --force had jumped 5.4.21→8.1.4 and broken vite.config.ts's manualChunks syntax; rolled back package.json/package-lock.json, confirmed clean build, then shipped the fix. Both verified live via curl -I.

Deleted legacy _domainconnect.stluker.com DNS record — unused Squarespace Domain Connect CNAME, flagged as a "Domains without HSTS" finding. DNS is fully delegated to Cloudflare so Domain Connect was never in use; removed rather than fixed.

Verified zone-level Bot Fight Mode, AI Labyrinth, AI bot policies, and Security.txt all active on the stluker.com zone — confirmed adequate coverage for real custom domains, so the corresponding .pages.dev-specific findings are safe to archive as reviewed/accepted.
DNS record count down to 20 (from 22) after removing the dead PTO records and the legacy _domainconnect record.
HSTS enabled zone-wide on stluker.com — cleared 10 of 18 original Security Insights findings in one toggle, covering all *.stluker.com custom domains.
cron-job.org fully decommissioned — both jobs (stl-bucket refresh + keepalive) deleted; stl-dispatcher now handles both internally on its single daily cron.

Auth pattern standardized across all Workers — Authorization: Bearer <secret> header auth via shared src/auth.js, with query-string ?secret= explicitly rejected. Applies to any new Worker with a protected endpoint going forward.

Cron trigger inventory confirmed — 5/5 free-tier slots in use. surfflow-scoring flagged for deletion: the surfcam project it served is already gone, but the Worker/cron/KV binding cleanup that was supposed to happen alongside it never did.

stl-dispatcher fully verified end-to-end — keepalive.ok:true (real 200 from Supabase stl_issues query) and statusSync.ok:true (GitHub reconciliation committing, 5 stable findings). Chain of 4 distinct bugs found and fixed to get here: malformed Bearer header (PowerShell pipe added trailing newline — fixed by trimming both sides of the auth comparison), malformed SUPABASE_URL, placeholder text accidentally used as SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, and finally the real bug — keepalive was querying the bare /rest/v1/ schema root which requires service_role, not anon. Fixed by querying a real table (stl_issues) instead. Migration is complete; supabase-keepalive + stl-status-sync decommissioned.
stl-bucket Friday cron restored — 0 14 * * 5 confirmed live in CF dashboard (1 trigger registered). Newsletter ingestion is no longer at risk of a missed run.

stl-dispatcher deployed — consolidates supabase-keepalive + stl-status-sync into one Worker/one cron trigger, freeing a permanent CF cron slot (5/5 -> 4/5). Root cause of the earlier stl-bucket cron-sync failure was CF's account-wide 5-slot cap, not a token permission issue as first suspected (confirmed via wrangler whoami — token already had workers_scripts:write). Corrected in the record.

T-13 CLOSED: stl-bucket /refresh fully remediated — secret rotated, cron-job.org migrated from ?secret= query string to Authorization: Bearer header, /refresh handler now enforces via shared checkAuth() module (constant-time compare, rejects query-string attempts outright), public-facing query-string link removed from empty-state HTML. Verified: header auth returns 200, old query-string URL returns 401 Unauthorized. Fail-notifications also enabled on all cron-job.org jobs (keep alive, stl-bucket refresh) — first-failure alerts instead of only disable-after-26-failures.
SECURITY: stl-bucket /refresh secret found exposed in plaintext via query string in cron-job.org dashboard — rotation + header-based auth migration opened as critical task (T-13).
supabase-keepalive wrangler.jsonc/dashboard cron drift resolved — redeployed from repo, live trigger confirmed back to 0 0 */5 * * (midnight UTC), matching source.

supabase-keepalive cron fixed for real — root cause was cron-job.org sending a malformed header (key "bearer" with raw token as the value) instead of a proper "Authorization: Bearer <token>" header. Not a secret mismatch — the Jun 26 fix only added the header, not the correct format. Corrected, job re-enabled, verified 200 OK with fresh response (pinged_at 2026-06-30T17:52:07Z, crash_readings/fire-api endpoint confirmed reachable).
surfcam fully decommissioned — Worker, Pages app, both DNS records, and the surfflow-scoring cron trigger removed from Cloudflare. Cron capacity back to a clean 5/5 (no zombie trigger).
geoQ and Media Gallery removed from active tracking — not being pursued, dropped from sites/tasks/repo lists
family.stluker.com map toggle shipped — Leaflet.js list/map view live for all 123 parks, amenity-colored pins
stl-status-sync Worker deployed — auto-updates data.json daily from CF + GitHub APIs
CI/CD confirmed wired for stl-sports + stl-music; retire.stluker.com confirmed live
surfcam marked inactive — decommission task added (disable cron, remove DNS)
Cross-link nav updated — music.stluker.com added to all three sites
stl.stluker.com launched — STL Bucket List beehiiv auto-ingestion via CF cron + Claude Haiku + Supabase
Supabase keepalive fixed — Bearer auth added to cron-job.org request
stluker.com food pulse upgraded — v5 Edge Function, structured JSON, DM Sans font
music.stluker.com launched — STL/Music tracker (concerts, comedy, theater)
DMARC diagnosed — p=none monitoring working; null SPF on root domain recommended
status.stluker.com launched — Jira-inspired board; data.json separated from fire-intel
Davidson feedback mapped — Paul's Pick badge deployed; pokelab + iron-rails source recovered to GitHub
Cross-link nav added — stluker.com ↔ family ↔ sports (top banner + footer)
sports.stluker.com fully wired — stl-sports Worker, SPORTS_KV, ESPN + iCal feeds
stluker.com SEO rearchitect — 12 routed URLs, sitemap, GSC verified
